headline: Parliamentary standing committee on defence has never held a public hearing
context: The Parliamentary Select Committee on Defence and Home Affairs has never conducted a public hearing since its establishment, meeting only in closed sessions.

cards.big: Defence committee has held zero public hearings in its entire history
cards.sub: Parliamentary oversight of military and police operates entirely in secret

cards.big: Standing Order 77A allows committees to determine their own procedure including secrecy
cards.sub: No requirement for public hearings even on non-classified matters

cards.big: Defence and home affairs budget totalled RM42 billion in 2025
cards.sub: RM42 billion in public spending overseen by a committee that never meets publicly

cards.big: US Senate Armed Services Committee holds 40+ public hearings annually on defence matters
cards.sub: Effective defence oversight is compatible with national security — secrecy is not required for everything

cards.big: Not everything about defence is classified — hiding all oversight behind secrecy protects waste, not security
Should the defence committee be required to hold at least quarterly public hearings on non-classified matters?
